youtube_items.title: [YT] Dr. Deepika Chopra | The Power of Real Optimism | Talks at Google
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] Psychologist and visual imagery expert, Dr. Deepika Chopra,  known as the The Optimism Doctor®,  discusses her book "The Power of Real Optimism: A Practical, Science-Based Guide to Staying Resilient, Curious, and Open Even When Life Is Hard," which shows us how to build the kind of optimism that can actually withstand real life.

Optimism isn’t about pretending everything is fine. It’s about staying open and flexible—especially when it’s not. Dr. Chopra offers a radically different definition: o

youtube_items.title: [YT] Jeff Kinney | Diary of a Wimpy Kid: Partypooper | Talks at Google
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] New York Times bestselling author, Jeff Kinney, talks about "Partypooper" his global smash-hit series, "Diary of a Wimpy Kid" which has sold over 300 million copies!

In Partypooper ,Greg Heffley is planning the ultimate party—for himself. But when the guest list grows, the decorations flop, and the surprises backfire, this birthday bash turns into a total blowout—just not the kind Greg had in mind. 

Get the book here: https://goo.gle/4u1fu8j.  
Learn more about Jeff: https://wimpykid.com/. 

J

youtube_items.title: [YT] Susie Wolff | Driven | Talks at Google
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] F1 Academy’s Managing Director, former professional racing driver and one of the most respected women in motorsport Susie Wolff discusses her book Driven, an inside look at the competitive world of Formula One and trailblazer Susie Wolff's 20-year career, taking readers from the driver’s seat and the F1 paddock all the way to the future of the sport.

Driven is a thrilling story about the highest levels of driving―but more than that, it’s a story of perseverance bound to inspire anyone on or off

youtube_items.title: [YT] The AI Industry is Spending $10 Million Against One Guy?
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] The AI industry has pledged over $10 million to stop one congressional candidate, Alex Bores, from getting elected. Why? And is it going to work?

[The opinions expressed in this video are my own and do not reflect those of any employer or organisation.]

More info at https://ny12.org/

youtube_items.title: [YT] Apply for the Affine Superintelligence Alignment Seminar #shorts
[YT] Tech is Good, AI Will Be Different
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] Why is technology good? Are there any exceptions to the rule?

Check out AISafety.info!
http://aisafety.info

Sources:
Large Language Models can Strategically Deceive their Users when Put Under Pressure: https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.07590
https://www.apolloresearch.ai/research/our-research-on-strategic-deception-presented-at-the-uks-ai-safety-summit

Frontier Models are Capable of In-context Scheming: h

analysis.problem: The high cost of AI coding tools, such as Claude Code, is a barrier to accessibility for many developers
analysis.root_cause: The high development and maintenance costs of AI models, combined with the desire for profit by companies like Anthropic, lead to expensive pricing models for end-users
leverage_points: Companies developing AI coding tools, such as Anthropic
Investors and funding bodies that support these companies

proposed_actions.action: Develop and promote open-source or low-cost AI coding alternatives
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase the number of developers using affordable AI coding tools by 20% within the next 6 months

proposed_actions.action: Encourage companies to offer tiered pricing models or discounts for non-profit and educational use
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Achieve a 30% increase in the number of non-profit and educational organizations using AI coding tools within the next year

evidence: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month, while Goose offers similar functionality for free
Listen Labs raised $69M to scale AI customer interviews, indicating a growing market for AI-powered tools
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the broader issue of unequal access to technology and digital resources, which can exacerbate existing social and economic inequalities, ultimately affecting the dignity and well-being of individuals and communities
analysis.summary: The high cost of AI coding tools is a barrier to accessibility, but promoting open-source alternatives and encouraging companies to offer tiered pricing models can increase accessibility and promote more equitable use of AI technology

raw_items.title: The Download: AI bottleneck debates, and BCI trials take off
raw_items.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. raw_items.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
raw_items.summary: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. But Subquadratic has started to bring the receipts, sharing the…

raw_items.title: The inevitable weakness of metrics
raw_items.summary: There are plenty of useful things a metric can reveal. There are even more it can obscure or corrupt. It took me well over a decade of tracking my own life in ever greater detail to fully appreciate this duality, which probably reveals something about both me and the nature of measurement. Like a lot…

raw_items.title: Brain-computer interface trials are taking off
raw_items.summary: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He has now spent almost three years using a brain-computer interface (BCI) that enables…

raw_items.title: Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
raw_items.summary:
For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm.

At its annual I/O developer conference, Google announced a sweeping redesign of the search box itself — the liter

raw_items.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
raw_items.summary:
Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the limitations of legacy cloud infrastructure.

TQ Ventures led the round, with participation from FPV Ventures

raw_items.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
raw_items.summary:
The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive.

Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide. But its pricing — ranging from $20 to $200 per month depending on usage — has sparked a growing rebellion among the very programmers it aims

raw_items.summary: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options.
